title: APRA grills Westpac on SME unit woes
full_text:
Westpac's business banking chief, Paul Fowler, and a group of senior bankers are being subjected to detailed interviews by the prudential regulator, after a bombshell internal report identified unsatisfactory processes and oversight, and other deficiencies within the lender's small business division.
The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority has interviewed more than 20 Westpac bankers progressively over three days this week, people with knowledge of the inquiries said on the condition of anonymity because the investigation is confidential. The interviews included national general managers within the division and some state managers.
APRA's keen interest in Westpac forms part of its broader review into big bank lending practices and credit risk policies related to small and medium businesses. Other lenders, including National Australia Bank and Commonwealth Bank, have engaged or are scheduled to engage with APRA over small business lending, too.
An APRA spokesman declined to comment, as did the banks.
The latest internal review almost mirrored a string of issues identified within the same unit five years ago.

author: Joyce Moullakis
